mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid federal funds rate increases compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate for future cash flows, leading to negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand, driving earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding historical peaks signals supply chain bottlenecks and overheating, triggering inflation fears and negative equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: High INDPRO periods consistently yield positive forward returns over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth acceleration directly expands corporate revenue bases, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth acceleration correlates negatively with forward returns over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment dropping below the natural rate threshold triggers wage-price dynamics that boost consumer discretionary spending, driving short-term positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Sub-5% unemployment periods show no significant forward return premium relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with elevated federal funds rates indicate real rate compression and policy lag effects, reducing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation with high rates consistently yields positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing construction paired with robust employment growth signals sustained credit expansion and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: High housing starts with strong employment fail to predict positive equity returns in walk-forward tests.
